title: update-available notice is permanently dark on Codex — wrong manifest path degrades to silent 'no update', indistinguishable from up-to-date
evidence: _updatelib.installed_version(root) opens `os.path.join(root, \".claude-plugin\", \"plugin.json\")` (line 88) and returns None on any OSError/ValueError (lines 91-92, no breadcrumb). The ca-codex plugin ships its manifest at `.codex-plugin/plugin.json` (per inventory.md structure and confirmed on disk: plugins/ca-codex/.codex-plugin/plugin.json is the only plugin.json under plugins/ca-codex/ — there is no `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`). session-start.py's update_notice_line(plugin) (lines 494-505) calls installed_version(plugin), gets None, then notice_line(None, latest) -> update_available(None, latest) -> version_gt(None, latest) which returns False whenever either side's parse_version is None (_updatelib.py:118-120) -> notice_line returns None -> update_notice_line returns \"\" and session-start.py's `if _update: print(_update)` (session-start.py:645-647) never fires.
impact: The update-available-notifier (spec: update-available-notifier.md, a purpose-built signal) is silently and permanently non-functional on every Codex install, on every session, forever — not intermittently degraded. Because the failure path and the healthy-and-current path both resolve to the same observable output (nothing printed), a Codex-side maintainer has no way to tell 'you are running the latest version' from 'this check is broken and has never once evaluated correctly on this host'. This is a signal-on-failure gap, not a correctness bug in what gets checked: the entire function's contract is 'never crash, degrade to silence' (AC-3), which is the right posture for a transient network hiccup but hides a structural, permanent misconfiguration identically.
recommendation: Either (a) make installed_version's manifest lookup host-aware (check .codex-plugin/plugin.json when .claude-plugin/plugin.json is absent), restoring the actual comparison, or (b) if the two plugins are meant to have independent update tracks, emit a WARN breadcrumb (once, non-blocking) when installed_version resolves to None on a plugin that IS present on disk, so a permanently-broken check doesn't read the same as 'nothing to report'.
acceptance_criteria: A Codex install with a genuinely newer release available surfaces the update notice (or, if update tracks are deliberately host-scoped, some explicit signal distinguishes 'checked, current' from 'could not check').
A regression test exercises _updatelib.installed_version against a ca-codex-shaped plugin directory layout (.codex-plugin/plugin.json only) — no such case exists in test_codex_adapter.py today.
